Title: Emotion-Driven Debt Collection Optimizer
Product Description: A UK-based collection FinTech develops a system to analyze live customer audio streams during outbound telephone collection calls. The AI maps voice patterns to detect emotional distress, vulnerability, or frustration, and dynamically switches automated Interactive Voice Response (IVR) scripts to exploit the debtor's psychological state and maximize immediate repayment rates.
Expected Role: Provider (Developing and selling the system under their own trademark).
Notes: Triggers the Article 5 ban on AI systems that deploy subliminal, manipulative, or deceptive techniques to distort a person's behavior in a way that causes psychological or physical harm, as well as the ban on emotion recognition systems within commercial or workplace environments used to manipulate interactions.

Title: Alternate Social Credit-Driven Overdraft Gate
Product Description: A London-based challenger bank builds an automated engine that scraped public social media interactions, political forum posts, and community forum reporting to generate an alternate "Social Trust and Reliability Score." This score is used as an absolute, automated gatekeeper to revoke or approve consumer retail overdraft protections.
Expected Role: Provider (Built in-house for their own infrastructure).
S5 (Integration): 90% (Deeply integrated into core retail banking ledgers).
Notes: Triggers the Article 5 ban on AI systems that deploy subliminal, manipulative, or deceptive techniques to distort a person's behavior in a way that causes psychological or physical harm, as well as the ban on emotion recognition systems within commercial or workplace environments used to manipulate interactions.

Title: B2B Automated Invoice Factoring Underwriter
Product Description: A retail bank builds a machine learning scoring engine to evaluate the creditworthiness of small UK businesses applying for fast invoice factoring lines. The model runs automated calculations on past transaction volumes, credit history, and supplier data to make binding credit line approvals or denials.
Expected Role: Provider (Built internally to put into service on their own platform).
Notes: Falls directly under Annex III, Point 5(b): AI systems intended to be used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons or establish their credit score (extended here via small business structures affecting individuals/sole traders).

Title: AML Alert Triage Optimization Engine
Product Description: A high-street bank in the UK purchases an AI model from a US vendor to ingest millions of clearing house events, score them, and dynamically filter out low-level Anti-Money Laundering (AML) transaction alerts before they can reach human analyst desks.
Expected Role: Deployer (Using a third-party commercial AI in a professional capacity).
S2 (Supply Chain): 100% (Purchased from an external provider with vendor terms).
S5 (Integration): 100% (Integrated directly into the core AML monitoring stack).
Notes: Classified under Annex III (Administration of Justice/Essential Infrastructure touchpoints) as it automates systemic security triage impacting access to financial system integrity and individual financial profiling.

Title: Engineering Recruitment & CV Ranking Pipeline
Product Description: A London quantitative trading platform designs an AI tool to parse and rank candidate CVs, GitHub commit records, and coding exercise scripts to select the top 2% of applicants for their graduate scheme.
Expected Role: Provider (Built proprietary pipeline for corporate use).
S3 (Autonomy): 80% (Ranks and filters; human picks from the top tier, but low scores are hidden).
Notes: Falls perfectly under Annex III, Point 4(a): AI systems intended to be used for the recruitment or selection of natural persons, notably for advertising vacancies, screening or filtering applications, and evaluating candidates.

Title: Front-Line Mortgage Rate Retail Chatbot
Product Description: A conversational LLM application deployed on a bank’s public mobile app. It answers basic user questions about mortgage interest rates, branch hours, and documentation requirements. It does not perform underwriting or log customer financial decisions.
Expected Role: Provider (White-labeling a basic API but offering it under their trademark).
S1 (Purpose/Domain): 100% (Informational client services; explicitly excludes credit risk evaluation).
S2 (Supply Chain): 80% (Calls external foundational model but wraps it locally).
S3 (Autonomy): 100% (Outputs conversations directly to retail users in real time).
S5 (Integration): 30% (Isolated web frontend, no backend ledger hooks).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(1): Providers must ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed so users are explicitly informed they are interacting with AI.

Title: Synthetic Document Generator for KYC Stress-Testing
Product Description: A FinTech platform utilizes generative adversarial models (GANs) to manufacture thousands of highly realistic, completely fake UK passports and utility bills to stress-test their automated Know-Your-Customer (KYC) image processing software.
Expected Role: Provider (Generating synthetic media for internal software validation).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(2): AI systems that generate synthetic audio, image, video, or text content must ensure outputs are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated.

Title: Generative Personal Finance Blog Copywriter
Product Description: A wealth management firm uses an LLM API to write weekly educational articles on "Understanding ISA Tax Allowances" which are published directly to their public-facing corporate blog to drive traffic.
Expected Role: Deployer (Operating an external generative system to publish content).
S3 (Autonomy): 50% (AI writes drafts, marketing team copyedits before publishing).
S4 (Interface/Modality): 100% (Text generation published to the public web).
S5 (Integration): 10% (Connected only to a content management system like WordPress).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(4): Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text published to inform the public on matters of public interest must disclose that the text has been artificially generated.

Title: Automated Personalized Account Summary Generator
Product Description: An app that runs an LLM to take raw monthly transaction lists and summarize them into a friendly, narrative email statement (e.g., "Looks like you spent a bit more on artisan coffee in Shoreditch this month!") sent directly to the customer.
Expected Role: Provider (Packaging the presentation feature within their financial application).
Notes: Requires disclosure under Article 50(1) and 50(2) because it generates synthetic text tailored directly to individuals.

Title: Inbound Support Email Sentiment Classifier
Product Description: A tool that reads incoming customer service emails to classify whether the tone is "Angry," "Neutral," or "Satisfied" purely to route priority cases to senior staff faster. It has no impact on contract terms, credit status, or employee tracking.
Expected Role: Provider (Built and integrated into internal CRM).
Notes: Categorized as an emotion classification system under Article 50(3). The deployer must inform the natural persons exposed to the system of its operation, provided it does not cross into prohibited workplace monitoring fields.

Title: Synthetic Customer Explainer Avatars
Product Description: A FinTech platform generates digital video avatars to explain personal pension restructuring options to users instead of recording live human actors.
Expected Role: Deployer (Using a synthetic video generation software provider).
Notes: Triggers strict deepfake/synthetic media transparency rules under Article 50(2); the video must prominently display an indicator that the avatar is artificial.

Title: Synthetic Market Tick-Data Generator
Product Description: An algo-trading startup uses deep generative modeling to create artificial stock market transaction ticks to test their trading execution latency speeds.
Expected Role: Provider (Created the generator for engineering data runs).
Notes: Requires machine-readable metadata marking under Article 50(2) to guarantee that synthetic database assets are never accidentally mixed into live production or market reporting streams.

Title: Core Ledger Database Load Balancer
Product Description: A machine learning system that analyzes processing bottlenecks across core banking SQL clusters and automatically shifts server loads between primary and backup infrastructure to keep processing times steady.
Expected Role: Provider (Built for internal cloud database optimization).
S4 (Interface/Modality): 0% (No human interface; background telemetry data only).
Notes: Excluded from Chapters II, III, and IV. No high-risk sector exposure, no human interaction, and no content generation. Free to operate with no legislative constraints.

Title: Retro Arcade Game Puck Physics Controller
Product Description: A UK developer designs an 8-bit retro arcade hockey game ("Breakaway") utilizing Matter.js to map simple velocity vector changes and puck collisions on an isolated local canvas loop.
Expected Role: Provider (Recreational hobbyist software).
Notes: Completely benign, out-of-scope entertainment system with zero intersection with high-stakes socioeconomic or corporate workflows.

Title: Corporate Email Junk/Spam Classifier
Product Description: Deploys a standard Bayesian and neural-network-based internal corporate email spam filter across employee mailboxes to isolate unsolicited commercial messages.
Expected Role: Deployer (Standard administrative business software).
Notes: Standard corporate background filtering application. Explicitly recognized as minimal/no risk by the European Commission.

Title: Open Banking Vulnerability Scanner
Product Description: A deeply detailed technical manual outlining a Python script that leverages an LLM to scan Open Banking API routing tables for broken object-level authorization vulnerabilities, mapping payload structures and outputting an exploit probability report.
Expected Role: Unknown (Unclear if it is an internal developer tool or an enterprise product).
S1 (Purpose/Domain): 40% (Clear technical action, but absolute zero sector or application context).
S3 (Autonomy): 60% (Generates a report, but workflow destination is hidden).
S5 (Integration): 0% (Deployment host architecture is a complete vacuum).
Notes: High technical density but zero regulatory signals. If this tool is used for internal corporate R&D, it is Exempt. If it is integrated into a live pipeline for a nuclear power plant's API architecture, it is High Risk. If sold as a standard standalone code checking application, it is Minimal Risk.

Title: Multi-Dimensional Behavioral Clustering Engine
Product Description: Detailed mathematical outline of an unsupervised K-means clustering algorithm checking streaming server access logs to flag deviations in database retrieval speeds across 12 distinct performance vector indices.
Expected Role: Unknown.
Notes: Complete absence of contextual intent. If the database logs contain consumer bank transaction histories to flag credit defaults, it is High Risk. If it tracks temperature patterns on hardware server chips, it is Minimal Risk.

Title: Structuring JSON Metadata Transformer
Product Description: Comprehensive API endpoint schema mapping an LLM task designed to ingest complex multi-page PDF documents, identify name/value pairs, and output cleanly formatted schema-validated JSON outputs to an AWS S3 bucket.
Expected Role: Unknown.
Notes: Missing intent and value chain positioning. The text details how the data changes shape, but completely masks what the data represents (e.g., medical diagnostics vs. public bank holiday calendars).

Title: High-Throughput Real-Time Stream Dashboard
Product Description: Front-end configuration file detailing a 3D visualization dashboard using Three.js and an interactive text window to summarize high-volume event data streaming at up to 10,000 queries per second.
Expected Role: Unknown.
Notes: Strong interface signal (S4 is 100%) but total failure on governance context. The model cannot determine if the dashboard is monitoring traffic safety systems or basic video game servers.

Title: Transactional Database Generator Profile
Product Description: Dense technical guide for configuring an AI script to review core banking ledgers and output mock records with matching mathematical balances for regression tracking.
Expected Role: Unknown.
Notes: Missing the distribution channel context. If this system is locked internally for developer tests, it is Exempt. If packaged as a commercial dataset generator for third parties, it triggers Limited Risk obligations.
